microsoft_ft: Owned end-to-end product strategy for Azure's **integration ecosystem** with enterprise partners (Databricks, Walmart, SAP, NetApp), standardizing connector workflows to sustain 45+ annual resilience drills and drive **$14M+ business impact**.

Led 0→1 build of an internal **Resilience Automation Platform**, defining self-service scheduling APIs and connector framework requirements that reduced operational toil by 30% and scaled execution without headcount growth.

Directed 14 cross-org recovery executions under executive visibility, including bridge lead for a sovereign-cloud **network isolation test** tied to a **$1.5B+ enterprise contract**, coordinating auth, permissioning, and data-sync dependencies.

Pioneered Azure's first proactive resilience testing capability, delivering a rack-level drill program in 4 months with a **94% recovery rate**, surfacing critical hardware defects and establishing a reusable platform model for continuous validation.

Scaled team leverage via an internal **AI agent** that restructured drill-planning workflows, cutting planning cycle time by 39% and increasing drill capacity by 21% — demonstrating agentic AI applied to enterprise-scale operations.
microsoft_2023: Drove **adoption of AI-powered code generation** workflows across 14 Azure service teams through targeted demos and enablement sessions, saving 37 engineering hours monthly and establishing measurable retention metrics.

Shaped the product roadmap by conducting **11+ user research interviews** with Azure service teams to surface critical integration gaps, directly influencing prioritization of intent-based YAML generation capabilities.

Improved developer experience by migrating documentation to an **AI-powered semantic search** tool with rigorous metadata standards, cutting lookup time by 83% and accelerating time-to-stand-up for new integrations.
microsoft_2022: Generated **$3M in accelerated revenue** and launched Azure regions 28% faster by driving cross-functional alignment on a unified automation prioritization framework spanning 140+ teams — directly informing roadmap sequencing.

Defined a platform-level product strategy to eliminate operational toil during region launches, conducting discovery with 20+ service teams to map **81 hours of manual effort per region** and building the automation roadmap.

Instrumented a **Power BI analytics dashboard** tracking operational toil and automation coverage across 140+ teams, enabling leadership to prioritize high-impact integration gaps with data-driven precision.
amazon_robotics: Achieved **zero operational downtime** during a 2,000+ unit pilot by owning the legacy OS migration strategy and mapping integration dependencies across 1,200+ stations — balancing first-party and partner tradeoffs at scale.

Partnered with IT, Operations, and Engineering to implement **automated CI/CD pipelines**, accelerating the software deployment cycle by 25% and establishing reusable infrastructure for future integration rollouts.

Facilitated Agile ceremonies and **backlog prioritization** to resolve 40+ high-priority tickets, stabilizing the developer intake process and improving cross-functional execution velocity across three teams.
pro_painters: Increased job bookings by **26%** by redesigning the end-to-end CRM workflow for 200+ monthly proposals, improving data integrity and enabling analytics-driven tracking of conversion and retention metrics.

Reduced Customer Acquisition Cost by **13%** and boosted conversions by 2.7% by executing a digital-first go-to-market strategy, optimizing web presence and iterating rapidly on channel performance data.

Identified and closed margin leaks by conducting **profitability analysis** across 1,000+ project records, delivering actionable pricing model improvements to leadership anchored to measurable revenue outcomes.

tailoring_notes: Wove 'connector framework' and 'integration ecosystem' (JD: connector framework, integration catalogue) into microsoft_ft bullets 1 and 2 to mirror North's platform vocabulary.

Used 'auth, permissioning, and data-sync' (JD: auth and identity, data sync, permissioning) explicitly in microsoft_ft bullet 3 to signal technical fluency with the platform capabilities Cohere needs built.

Introduced 'agentic AI applied to enterprise-scale operations' (JD: Agentic AI domain) in microsoft_ft bullet 5 and 'AI-powered semantic search' in microsoft_2023 bullet 3 to hit the AI-first PM and agentic AI requirements.
Reframed microsoft_2022 bullet 3 around 'instrumented analytics' and 'adoption gaps' (JD: instrument analytics to track adoption, time-to-stand-up, retention) to surface metrics-first orientation.
Trimmed to role minimums or one above across smaller roles to resolve page overflow while preserving all JD-critical substance.
